docs.title: 3.1 Communication USB
docs.text:
Connecteur 4 pins 
    │
    ├── GPIO15 ←→ TBD
    ├── GPIO16 ←→ TBD
    ├── GPIO17 ←→ TBD
    └── GPIO18 ←→ TBD

Connecteur USB (direct)
    ├── GPIO4  ←→ SDA (I2C0)
    ├── GPIO5  ←→ SCL (I2C0)
    ├── GPIO19 ──► CLK ext
    └── RUN    ◄── Reset MCU
  • GPIO15/18 : signaux non encore définis sur le connecteur 4 pins
  • GPIO16/17 : signaux non encore définis sur le connecteur 4 pins
  • GPIO4/5 : I2C0 — bus accessible depuis le connecteur USB
  • GPIO19 : horloge externe USB
  • RUN : reset hardware du RP2040 depuis le connecteur USB

docs.title: 3.2 Pt1000 — Multiplexeur analogique
docs.text:
Architecture : MCU → MUX (8 canaux) → Pt1000 (1 parmi 8) → Ampli-op → MCU (ADC)

RP2040
  │
  ├── GPIO0 (A0) ─┐
  ├── GPIO1 (A1) ─┼──► MUX 8:1 ──► Pt1000 sélectionné (S1 … S8)
  ├── GPIO2 (A2) ─┘         │
  │                         └──► Ampli-op ──► GPIO26 (ADC0)
  └── GPIO3 ────────────────────► Switch ON/OFF (tous les Pt1000)

Logique de sélection (A2:A1:A0) :

A2 A1 A0 Pt1000 sélectionné 0 0 0 S1 0 0 1 S2 0 1 0 S3 0 1 1 S4 1 0 0 S5 1 0 1 S6 1 1 0 S7 1 1 1 S8
  • GPIO3 met tous les Pt1000 hors tension (LOW = OFF) — permet d'économiser l'énergie entre mesures
  • GPIO26 (ADC0) lit la tension de sortie de l'ampli-op proportionnelle à la résistance du Pt1000 actif
  • La conversion résistance → température se fait en firmware (courbe de calibration Pt1000 standard)

docs.title: 3.3 Altimètre — MS5607-02BA03-50 (TE Connectivity)
docs.text:
Communication exclusivement en SPI mode 0 (CPOL=0, CPHA=0) à 1 MHz :

RP2040                         MS5607
  │                               │
  ├── GPIO10 (SCLK) ─────────────►│ SCLK
  ├── GPIO11 (SDI / MOSI) ───────►│ SDI    (commandes MCU → capteur)
  ├── GPIO8  (SDO / MISO) ◄───────│ SDO    (données capteur → MCU)
  └── GPIO9  (CSB)  ─────────────►│ CS     (actif bas)

Commandes firmware (OSR = 4096, précision maximale) :

Commande Octet Délai Usage Reset 0x1E 3 ms Initialisation obligatoire Convert D1 (P) 0x48 10 ms Lance conversion pression Convert D2 (T) 0x58 10 ms Lance conversion température ADC Read 0x00 — Lit résultat 24 bits PROM Read C_i 0xA0 + 2i — Lit coefficient calibration

Séquence d'initialisation : 1. CS haut → Reset → attendre 3 ms 2. Lire PROM : 8 mots de 16 bits (C0…C7), vérifier C1…C6 ≠ 0

Cycle de mesure : 1. Conv D1 → attendre 10 ms → ADC Read → D1 (pression brute) 2. Conv D2 → attendre 10 ms → ADC Read → D2 (température brute) 3. Compensation 2nd ordre (algo datasheet MS5607 rev. 1.2) → P (hPa), T (°C) 4. Altitude : 44330 × (1 − (P / P₀)^0.190295) m

Plages de mesure : - Pression : 10 – 1200 hPa - Température : −40°C à +85°C

docs.title: 3.4 IMU — ICM-20948 (Adafruit #4554)
docs.text:
9 axes : accéléromètre + gyroscope (ICM-20948) + magnétomètre AK09916 intégré. Bus I2C1 à 400 kHz (Wire1, GPIO6/7) :

RP2040                         ICM-20948
  │                               │
  ├── GPIO6  (SDA) ◄─────────────►│ SDA
  ├── GPIO7  (SCL) ──────────────►│ SCL
  ├── GPIO12 (AD0) ──────────────►│ AD0    (LOW → adresse I2C 0x68)
  ├── GPIO13       ◄──────────────│ INT1   (actif bas — données prêtes)
  └── GPIO14       ──────────────►│ FSYNC  (latch accéléro sur front montant)

Détection firmware : - Registre WHO_AM_I (0x00, bank 0) → réponse attendue 0xEA

Paramètres d'initialisation (Adafruit ICM20X) :

Paramètre Valeur configurée Plage possible Plage accéléro ±4 g ±2 / ±4 / ±8 / ±16 g Plage gyroscope ±500 °/s ±250 / ±500 / ±1000 / ±2000 °/s Fréquence magnéto 10 Hz (AK09916) 10 / 20 / 50 / 100 Hz

Signaux de contrôle : - INT1 (GPIO13, actif bas) : déclenche une interruption quand un échantillon est prêt — permet la lecture sans polling - FSYNC (GPIO14) : synchronise le timestamp accéléro sur un événement externe (GPS PPS, caméra…) - AD0 (GPIO12) : fixé LOW au démarrage → adresse fixe 0x68, ne change pas en cours d'exécution

Données produites : - Accélération 3 axes (g) - Vitesse angulaire 3 axes (°/s) - Champ magnétique 3 axes (µT) — AK09916 via bus I2C secondaire interne - Température interne (°C)

Représentation d'attitude — quaternion unitaire q = [w, x, y, z] :

Les données brutes des 3 capteurs sont fusionnées pour produire un quaternion représentant la rotation du repère corps vers le repère de référence :

Source Rôle dans la fusion Gyroscope (°/s) Intégration de la vitesse angulaire → propagation du quaternion à haute fréquence Accéléromètre (g) Correction du tangage et du roulis à basse fréquence (référence gravité) Magnétomètre (µT) Correction du lacet (référence champ magnétique terrestre)

Le quaternion évite le blocage de cardan (gimbal lock) inhérent aux angles d'Euler. L'algorithme de fusion (filtre complémentaire, Madgwick ou Mahony) sera sélectionné après caractérisation des capteurs.

docs.title: 4.1 Principe
docs.text:
Chaque capteur est un module totalement indépendant. L'absence d'un capteur sur le PCB ne doit jamais provoquer de crash ou de blocage. Le firmware détecte la présence de chaque capteur à l'initialisation et désactive silencieusement les modules absents.

main.cpp
  │
  ├── sensor_manager        ← orchestre les modules, aucune logique capteur ici
  │     ├── imu_module      ← optionnel
  │     ├── altimeter_module← optionnel
  │     ├── pt1000_module   ← optionnel
  │     └── usb_module      ← optionnel
  │
  └── data_bus             ← structure de données partagée (lecture seule depuis les modules)

docs.title: 4.2 Interface commune (SensorModule)
docs.text:
V2 — rupture avec le code existant. L'ancienne classe Sensor (begin() / update() / getData()) est remplacée par SensorModule. Toutes les implémentations V2 dérivent exclusivement de SensorModule.

Chaque module implémente la même interface minimaliste :

class SensorModule {
public:
    virtual bool        init()         = 0;  // false si capteur absent/défaillant
    virtual bool        read()         = 0;  // false si lecture échoue
    virtual bool        isReady() const = 0;
    virtual const char* name()    const = 0;
    virtual ~SensorModule() {}
};

Règles : - init() ne bloque jamais si le capteur ne répond pas (timeout ≤ 50 ms) - read() ne bloque jamais (retourne false si donnée non disponible) - Aucun module n'appelle un autre module directement

docs.title: 4.2.1 Codes d'erreur
docs.text:
init() et read() retournent un bool pour la compatibilité avec SensorManager, mais chaque module doit exposer un code d'erreur détaillé via lastError() :

enum class SensorError : uint8_t {
    OK           = 0,
    NOT_FOUND,       // pas de réponse sur le bus (I2C NACK, SPI timeout)
    BAD_ID,          // WHO_AM_I inattendu ou CRC PROM invalide
    DATA_INVALID,    // valeur hors plage physique
    READ_FAIL        // lecture partielle ou corrompue
};

docs.title: 4.2.2 Politique de récupération
docs.text:
Événement Réaction immédiate Réaction après 3 échecs consécutifs init() → false module marqué non-prêt, log Serial aucune — le module reste désactivé read() → false donnée ignorée, valid = false notification via WatchdogComm (USB) Bus I2C bloqué reset du bus (Wire.end() / Wire.begin()) module désactivé définitivement

Watchdog externe : le module watchdog est un système séparé, codé indépendamment, qui communique avec l'ADCS via USB. WatchdogComm est l'interface côté ADCS qui gère cette liaison — réception des données du watchdog et émission de notifications d'état. Le protocole USB applicatif est défini du côté du module watchdog.

docs.title: 4.3 Gestionnaire de capteurs (SensorManager)
docs.text:
class SensorManager {
    SensorModule* modules[MAX_MODULES];
    uint8_t       count;
public:
    void registerModule(SensorModule* m);
    void initAll();   // init() sur chacun, log les absents
    void readAll();   // read() sur les présents seulement
};
  • initAll() itère sur les modules enregistrés, marque isReady = false si init() échoue
  • readAll() ignore silencieusement les modules non prêts
  • main.cpp choisit quels modules enregistrer (via #define ENABLE_IMU, etc.)

docs.title: 4.4 Bus de données partagé
docs.text:
Structure en lecture seule entre modules (chaque module écrit uniquement sa propre section) :

struct AdcsData {
    struct {
        float ax, ay, az;       // m/s²
        float gx, gy, gz;       // °/s
        float mx, my, mz;       // µT
        bool  valid;
    } imu;

    struct {
        float pressure;         // hPa
        float temperature;      // °C
        float altitude;         // m
        bool  valid;
    } altimeter;

    struct {
        float temps[8];         // °C, index 0..7 = S1..S8
        bool  valid[8];
        uint8_t active_count;
    } pt1000;

    struct {
        bool  connected;
    } usb;
};

extern AdcsData g_adcs;

docs.title: 4.5 Configuration par #define
docs.text:
Dans config.h, chaque capteur est activable/désactivable à la compilation :

// Activer/désactiver les modules capteurs
#define ENABLE_IMU        1
#define ENABLE_ALTIMETER  1
#define ENABLE_PT1000     1
#define ENABLE_USB        1

// Nombre de Pt1000 connectés (1 à 8)
#define PT1000_COUNT  8

docs.title: 5. Flux d'exécution
docs.text:
setup()
  └── SensorManager::initAll()
        ├── IMU::init()        → détecte sur I2C 0x68/0x69, timeout 50 ms
        ├── Altimeter::init()  → détecte sur SPI (CSB GPIO9), timeout 50 ms
        ├── Pt1000::init()     → vérifie ADC GPIO26 lisible, teste GPIO0-3
        └── USB::init()        → vérifie GPIO4/5 bus I2C

loop()
  └── SensorManager::readAll()   ← exécuté à la fréquence souhaitée
        ├── IMU::read()          → lit via I2C → g_adcs.imu
        ├── Altimeter::read()    → lit via SPI → g_adcs.altimeter
        ├── Pt1000::read()       → séquence mux A0-A2, lit ADC → g_adcs.pt1000
        └── USB::read()          → sondage état bus → g_adcs.usb

docs.title: 6. Dépendances inter-modules
docs.text:
Aucune dépendance directe entre modules capteurs.

Module      Dépend de
──────────  ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
imu         GPIO6, GPIO7, GPIO12, GPIO13, GPIO14
altimeter   GPIO8 (SDO), GPIO9 (CSB), GPIO10 (SCLK), GPIO11 (SDI)
pt1000      GPIO0 (A0), GPIO1 (A1), GPIO2 (A2), GPIO3 (SW), GPIO26
usb         GPIO4, GPIO5, GPIO15–18, GPIO19, RUN
main        SensorManager, AdcsData (g_adcs)

Si un GPIO est absent ou en court-circuit, seul le module correspondant est marqué non prêt. Les autres continuent de fonctionner normalement.

docs.title: 7. Résumé des bus de communication
docs.text: Bus GPIOs Périphérique Vitesse / protocole I2C0 GPIO4, GPIO5 Connecteur USB (SDA/SCL) 400 kHz I2C1 GPIO6, GPIO7 IMU ICM-20948 (0x68) 400 kHz SPI GPIO8–11 Altimètre MS5607 (CS=GPIO9) 1 MHz, mode 0 (CPOL=0,CPHA=0) GPIO GPIO16, GPIO17 Connecteur 4 pins → USB (TBD) — ADC GPIO26 Pt1000 via Mux + Ampli-op 12 bits, Vref 3.3 V GPIO GPIO0–3 Mux A0/A1/A2 + Switch Pt1000 — GPIO GPIO12 IMU AD0 (adresse I2C fixe) — GPIO GPIO13 IMU INT1 (interruption données prêtes) actif bas GPIO GPIO14 IMU FSYNC (latch accéléro) front montant GPIO GPIO15, GPIO18 Connecteur 4 pins → USB (TBD) —

docs.title: 8.1 Librairies externes — PlatformIO (lib_deps)
docs.text:
Librairie Version Rôle dans le firmware Adafruit ICM20X ^2.0.5 Driver ICM-20948 : lecture accéléro / gyro / magnéto Adafruit BusIO ^1.14.1 Abstraction I2C et SPI — dépendance d'ICM20X Adafruit Unified Sensor ^1.1.9 Interface capteur générique — dépendance d'ICM20X

Adafruit BusIO et Adafruit Unified Sensor sont des dépendances transitives : elles sont installées automatiquement par PlatformIO avec Adafruit ICM20X.

docs.title: 8.5 Notes sur les algorithmes embarqués (sans librairie externe)
docs.text: Algorithme Module Norme / source Compensation pression/température 2nd ordre altimeter Datasheet MS5607 rev. 1.2 Équation maison tension → T pt1000 Calibration propre au circuit (ampli-op + Mux) — remplace Callendar-Van Dusen Loi barométrique P → altitude altimeter Formule hypsométrique standard Fusion IMU → quaternion imu Algorithme à définir après caractérisation capteurs (Madgwick / Mahony / complémentaire) Filtres numériques tous modules À ajouter après tests sur capteurs réels (passe-bas, moyenne glissante…)

docs.title: 3.1 External Communication — Watchdog
docs.text:
Watchdog module
    ├── GPIO4  ←→ SDA (I2C0 — address 0x55)
    ├── GPIO5  ←→ SCL (I2C0)
    └── GPIO19 ──► CLK output (500 µs half-period, 1 kHz)
  • GPIO4/5: I2C0 — ADCS communicates with the external watchdog over this bus (Wire, default Mbed pins for Pico)
  • GPIO19: clock output pulsed once per read() cycle; idles LOW
  • ADCS → Watchdog: one status byte per loop cycle via sendStatus():
  • Bit 0 (WD_STATUS_IMU_OK): g_adcs.imu.valid
  • Bit 1 (WD_STATUS_ALT_OK): g_adcs.altimeter.valid
  • Bit 2 (WD_STATUS_PT_OK): g_adcs.pt1000.active_count > 0
  • Bits 3–7: reserved
  • RUN: hardware reset of the RP2040 from an external source

docs.title: 3.2 Pt1000 — Analog Multiplexer
docs.text:
Sensor: PTFC102B1G0 — TE Connectivity (PTF family)

Parameter Value Nominal resistance R₀ 1000 Ω at 0 °C Temperature coefficient 3850 ppm/K (α₀…₁₀₀) Tolerance (Class B) ±(0.30 + 0.005 × |T|) °C Operating range −50 °C to +600 °C

Architecture: MCU → MUX (8:1) → Pt1000 (1 of 8) → Op-amp → MCU (ADC)

RP2040
  │
  ├── GPIO0 (A0) ─┐
  ├── GPIO1 (A1) ─┼──► MUX 8:1 ──► Selected Pt1000 (S1 … S8)
  ├── GPIO2 (A2) ─┘         │
  │                         └──► Op-amp ──► GPIO26 (ADC0)
  └── GPIO3 ────────────────────► Switch ON/OFF (all Pt1000s)

Selection logic (A2:A1:A0):

A2 A1 A0 Selected Pt1000 0 0 0 S1 0 0 1 S2 0 1 0 S3 0 1 1 S4 1 0 0 S5 1 0 1 S6 1 1 0 S7 1 1 1 S8
  • GPIO3 powers all Pt1000s OFF (LOW) between measurements — saves power
  • GPIO26 (ADC0) samples the op-amp output; ADC resolution fixed to 10 bits (analogReadResolution(10))
  • MUX settling delay: 500 µs after each channel switch before sampling (to be refined after hardware characterization)

Voltage → temperature conversion — calibration LUT:

Source file: Vout_vs_T_20260603_180610.csv — 200 measured points, steady-state op-amp output at GPIO26 as a function of temperature.

LUT_V[200]   float array, −0.0145 V … 2.3058 V
T_CAL_MIN  = −60 °C
T_CAL_MAX  = +80 °C
T_CAL_STEP = (80 − (−60)) / 199 ≈ 0.7035 °C/step

Lookup: binary-search floor (no interpolation). v → lo such that LUT_V[lo] ≤ v < LUT_V[lo+1] → T = T_CAL_MIN + lo × T_CAL_STEP

Readings outside [LUT_V[0], LUT_V[199]] are rejected (valid[ch] = false).

docs.title: 3.3 Altimeter — MS5607-02BA03-50 (TE Connectivity)
docs.text:
Hardware target: SPI on GPIO8–11 (see GPIO map).

Current firmware implementation: I2C, via the bundled MS5607 library (Libnexamples/MS5607-master/). The available library uses Wire internally; the SPI pins (GPIO8–11) are reserved on the PCB but not driven by the current firmware. A SPI-based driver can be substituted in altimeter.h/.cpp when available without any other change.

RP2040 (hardware target)       MS5607
  │
  ├── GPIO10 (SCLK) ─────────►│ SCLK
  ├── GPIO11 (SDI / MOSI) ───►│ SDI
  ├── GPIO8  (SDO / MISO) ◄───│ SDO
  └── GPIO9  (CSB)  ─────────►│ CS

Initialization: _sensor.begin() — returns false if sensor not found.

Measurement cycle: 1. _sensor.readDigitalValue() → triggers ADC conversion; returns false on failure 2. _sensor.getTemperature() → T (°C) 3. _sensor.getPressure() → P (hPa) 4. _sensor.getAltitude() → altitude (m)

Validation ranges (firmware-enforced): - Pressure: 10 – 1200 hPa - Temperature: −40 °C to +85 °C

Values outside these ranges set valid = false and increment the failure counter.

docs.title: 3.4 IMU — ICM-20948 (Adafruit #4554)
docs.text:
9-axis IMU: accelerometer + gyroscope (ICM-20948) + integrated AK09916 magnetometer. I2C1 bus at 400 kHz, GPIO6/7.

Implementation note: Wire1 is not exported by the Arduino-Mbed variant for the Pico. A custom instance is created in imu.cpp: static TwoWire _wire1(PIN_IMU_SDA, PIN_IMU_SCL);

RP2040                         ICM-20948
  │
  ├── GPIO6  (SDA) ◄──────────►│ SDA
  ├── GPIO7  (SCL) ────────────►│ SCL
  └── GPIO12 (AD0) ────────────►│ AD0   (LOW → I2C address 0x68)

Initialization: 1. AD0 (GPIO12) driven LOW → address locked to 0x68, never changes at runtime 2. _wire1.begin() + _wire1.setClock(400000UL) 3. _icm.begin_I2C(0x68, &_wire1) — returns false if not found 4. Range configuration: - Accelerometer: ±4 g (ICM20948_ACCEL_RANGE_4_G) - Gyroscope: ±500 dps (ICM20948_GYRO_RANGE_500_DPS) - Magnetometer data rate: 10 Hz (AK09916_MAG_DATARATE_10_HZ)

Measurement cycle: 1. _icm.getEvent(&accel, &gyro, &temp, &mag) → fills sensor events 2. accel.acceleration.x/y/z → m/s² 3. gyro.gyro.x/y/z → rad/s 4. mag.magnetic.x/y/z → µT

Read validation: if the acceleration magnitude squared equals zero, the read is treated as failed (DATA_INVALID). Three consecutive failures trigger a watchdog status notification.

Attitude representation — unit quaternion q = [w, x, y, z]:

Source Role in fusion Gyroscope (°/s) Integrates angular rate → propagates quaternion at high frequency Accelerometer (g) Corrects pitch and roll at low frequency (gravity reference) Magnetometer (µT) Corrects yaw (Earth magnetic field reference)

Fusion algorithm (complementary filter, EKF, Madgwick…) to be selected after sensor characterization.

docs.title: 4.1 Principle
docs.text:
Each sensor is a fully independent module. The absence of a sensor never causes a crash or a hang. The firmware detects each sensor at initialization and silently disables absent modules.

main.cpp
  │
  ├── SensorManager        ← orchestrates modules, holds no sensor logic
  │     ├── ImuModule      ← optional
  │     ├── AltimeterModule← optional
  │     ├── Pt1000Module   ← optional
  │     └── WatchdogComm   ← optional
  │
  └── AdcsData g_adcs      ← shared data bus (each module writes only its section)

docs.title: 4.2 Common Interface (SensorModule)
docs.text:
Each module implements the same minimal interface:

class SensorModule {
public:
    virtual bool        init()            = 0;  // false if sensor absent/faulty
    virtual bool        read()            = 0;  // false if read fails
    virtual bool        isReady()   const = 0;
    virtual const char* name()      const = 0;
    virtual SensorError lastError() const = 0;
    virtual ~SensorModule() {}
};

Rules: - init() never hangs if the sensor does not respond - read() never blocks; returns false if data unavailable - No module calls another module directly

docs.title: 4.2.1 Error Codes
docs.text:
enum class SensorError : uint8_t {
    OK           = 0,
    NOT_FOUND,       // no response on bus (I2C NACK, SPI timeout)
    BAD_ID,          // unexpected WHO_AM_I or invalid PROM CRC
    DATA_INVALID,    // value outside physical range
    READ_FAIL        // partial or corrupted read
};

docs.title: 4.2.2 Recovery Policy
docs.text:
Event Immediate reaction After 3 consecutive failures init() → false module marked not-ready, Serial log none — module stays disabled read() → false data ignored, valid = false WatchdogComm::sendStatus() flags the failed sensor Locked I2C bus bus reset (Wire.end() / Wire.begin()) module permanently disabled

External watchdog: WatchdogComm is the ADCS-side interface to the independent watchdog module. It communicates over I2C0 (GPIO4/5) and sends a CLK pulse (GPIO19) every loop cycle. The communication protocol is defined on the watchdog side.

docs.title: 4.3 Sensor Manager (SensorManager)
docs.text:
class SensorManager {
    SensorModule* modules[MAX_MODULES];   // MAX_MODULES = 4
    uint8_t       count;
public:
    void registerModule(SensorModule* m);
    void initAll();   // calls init() on each; logs absent modules
    void readAll();   // skips modules that are not ready
};
  • initAll() sets isReady = false for any module whose init() returns false
  • readAll() silently skips non-ready modules
  • main.cpp selects which modules to register via #define ENABLE_*

docs.title: 4.4 Shared Data Bus (AdcsData g_adcs)
docs.text:
Each module writes only its own section; main.cpp reads all sections.

struct AdcsData {
    struct {
        float ax, ay, az;       // m/s²
        float gx, gy, gz;       // rad/s
        float mx, my, mz;       // µT
        bool  valid;
    } imu;

    struct {
        float pressure;         // hPa
        float temperature;      // °C
        float altitude;         // m
        bool  valid;
    } altimeter;

    struct {
        float   temps[8];       // °C, index 0..7 = S1..S8
        bool    valid[8];
        uint8_t active_count;
    } pt1000;

    struct {
        bool connected;
    } watchdog;
};

extern AdcsData g_adcs;

docs.title: 4.5 Compile-time Configuration (config.h)
docs.text:
#define ENABLE_IMU        1   // set to 0 to exclude module entirely
#define ENABLE_ALTIMETER  1
#define ENABLE_PT1000     1
#define ENABLE_WATCHDOG   1

#define PT1000_COUNT      8   // number of Pt1000 sensors wired (1–8)
#define MAX_MODULES       4

docs.title: 5. Execution Flow
docs.text:
setup()
  ├── Serial.begin(115200)          — waits up to 3 s for USB host, then proceeds
  └── SensorManager::initAll()
        ├── ImuModule::init()        → AD0 LOW, _wire1.begin(), probe 0x68
        ├── AltimeterModule::init()  → _sensor.begin() (I2C, library-internal)
        ├── Pt1000Module::init()     → GPIO0-3 config, ADC smoke-test on GPIO26
        └── WatchdogComm::init()     → GPIO19 LOW, Wire.begin(), probe 0x55

loop()  [period = 1000 ms, paced via millis()]
  └── SensorManager::readAll()
        ├── ImuModule::read()        → getEvent() → g_adcs.imu
        ├── AltimeterModule::read()  → readDigitalValue() → g_adcs.altimeter
        ├── Pt1000Module::read()     → MUX scan × 8, 500 µs settle → g_adcs.pt1000
        └── WatchdogComm::read()     → tickClock() + I2C poll → g_adcs.watchdog
  │
  ├── WatchdogComm::sendStatus(byte)  — IMU/Alt/Pt1000 status bits → watchdog
  └── printAdcsStatus()               — Serial print of g_adcs

docs.title: 6. Inter-module Dependencies
docs.text:
No direct dependencies between sensor modules.

Module       Depends on
───────────  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ImuModule    GPIO6, GPIO7, GPIO12  (I2C1 — custom TwoWire instance)
Altimeter    MS5607 library (I2C internal)  [GPIO8-11 reserved / unused]
Pt1000       GPIO0, GPIO1, GPIO2, GPIO3, GPIO26  (ADC0)
WatchdogComm GPIO4, GPIO5 (I2C0 — Wire default), GPIO19
main         SensorManager, AdcsData (g_adcs)

If a GPIO is absent or short-circuited, only the corresponding module is marked not ready. All other modules continue normally.

docs.title: 7.5 Embedded Algorithms (no external library)
docs.text: Algorithm Module Notes LUT binary-search floor lookup pt1000 200-entry LUT_V[], lo + (hi-lo)/2 avoids uint16_t overflow Watchdog status byte encoding main / watchdog 3-bit field: IMU OK, Alt OK, Pt1000 active IMU sensor fusion → quaternion imu Algorithm TBD after characterization (Madgwick / Mahony / complementary) Digital filters all modules To be added after real sensor testing

docs.title: 7.6 Algorithm Complexity Analysis
docs.text: Convention: one "op" = one elementary CPU instruction (add, sub, mul, shift, compare, load/store). I2C ops count software-visible steps (register writes to the I2C controller); hardware bit-banging runs in parallel and is not counted as CPU ops.

docs.title: lutLookup — Binary-search floor (pt1000.cpp)
docs.text:
Operations per loop iteration:

Instruction Operation Count hi - lo > 1 subtraction + comparison 2 (hi - lo) / 2 subtraction + right shift 2 lo + mid addition 1 LUT_V[mid] array read 1 LUT_V[mid] <= v float comparison 1 lo = mid or hi = mid assignment 1

8 ops/iteration × ⌊log₂(199)⌋ = 7 iterations = 56 ops Final result: T_CAL_MIN + lo × T_CAL_STEP = 1 mul + 1 add = +2 ops

NOP(lutLookup) ≈ 58 (worst case 66 if 8 iterations) Time complexity: O(log N), N = 200

Memory:

Location Content Size Flash (static) LUT_V[200] — const float array 800 B Stack (temporary) lo, hi, mid (uint16_t) + v (float) 10 B Auxiliary O(1) — independent of N —

docs.title: Pt1000 full scan — 8 channels (Pt1000Module::read)
docs.text:
Per channel: selectChannel + analogRead + lutLookup + validation + write to g_adcs

Step NOP / channel × 8 channels selectChannel (3 × digitalWrite) 3 24 analogRead (ADC peripheral) 1 8 lutLookup 58 464 Vout calculation + range check + write 5 40

NOP(read) ≈ 536 per call Time complexity: O(N), N = PT1000_COUNT (8)

Memory:

Location Content Size Stack ch, raw, v, count ~9 B Output (g_adcs.pt1000) 8 × float + 8 × bool + uint8_t 41 B

docs.title: Watchdog — Wire I2C transactions + CLK + status encoding (main.cpp + watchdog.cpp)
docs.text:
Status byte encoding:

uint8_t status = 0;
if (imu.valid)               status |= 0x01;   // cmp + OR
if (altimeter.valid)         status |= 0x02;   // cmp + OR
if (pt1000.active_count > 0) status |= 0x04;   // cmp + OR
Step NOP 3 comparisons + 3 bitwise ORs + 1 init 7

tickClock() — GPIO19 pulse:

Step NOP digitalWrite(HIGH) + delayMicroseconds 2 digitalWrite(LOW) + delayMicroseconds 2 Subtotal 4

read() — I2C bus poll (Wire, I2C0):

Step NOP Wire.beginTransmission(0x55) — buffer setup 2 Wire.endTransmission() — START + addr + STOP 5 Subtotal 7

sendStatus() — I2C write:

Step NOP Wire.beginTransmission(0x55) 2 Wire.write(statusByte) — buffer 1 byte 1 Wire.endTransmission() — START + addr + data + STOP 5 Subtotal 8

NOP(Watchdog per cycle) = 7 + 4 + 7 + 8 = 26 Memory: O(1) — 1 B (status), I2C TX buffer managed by Wire library (~32 B static)

docs.title: IMU read — ImuModule::read + Adafruit ICM20X getEvent()
docs.text:
Firmware layer (imu.cpp):

Step NOP amag² zero-check (3 mul + 2 add + 1 cmp) 6 Copy 9 floats → g_adcs.imu 9 valid flag + fail counter 3 Subtotal firmware 18

Adafruit ICM20X library — getEvent() breakdown:

I2C transactions (I2C1, 400 kHz):

Transaction Bytes NOP Bank select write (REG_BANK_SEL) 1 3 Burst read: accel + gyro + temp (0x2D→0x3A) 14 16 Bank select for magnetometer passthrough 1 3 Trigger AK09916 read via I2C master 2 5 Read mag data (HX..HZ + status) 8 10 I2C subtotal 26 bytes 37

Data conversion (raw registers → physical units):

Conversion Operations NOP 3 × accel: 2-byte → int16 assembly (shift + OR) 3 × 2 6 3 × accel: int16 → float × sensitivity scale 3 × 2 6 3 × gyro: same 3 × 4 12 3 × mag: same 3 × 4 12 Temp: assembly + linear conversion 3 3 Conversion subtotal 39

NOP(getEvent) ≈ 37 + 39 = 76 NOP(ImuModule::read total) = 18 + 76 = 94

Memory:

Location Content Size Stack 4 × sensors_event_t (library) ~128 B Output (g_adcs.imu) 9 × float + bool 37 B

docs.title: Altimeter read — AltimeterModule::read + MS5607 library
docs.text:
Firmware layer (altimeter.cpp):

Step NOP 3 getters + 3 writes to g_adcs 6 Range validation (2 × 2 comparisons) 4 Subtotal firmware 10

MS5607 library — readDigitalValue() breakdown:

I2C transactions (I2C via Wire):

Transaction NOP Write: trigger D1 conversion command (OSR) 3 (hardware ADC conversion delay — not CPU ops) — Read ADC result D1 (3 bytes) 5 Write: trigger D2 conversion command 3 (hardware delay) — Read ADC result D2 (3 bytes) 5 I2C subtotal 16

Temperature compensation (MS5607 datasheet second-order formulas):

Formula Operations NOP dT = D2 - C5 × 2⁸ 1 shift + 1 sub 2 TEMP = 2000 + dT × C6 / 2²³ 1 mul + 1 shift + 1 add 3 OFF = C2 × 2¹⁷ + (C4 × dT) / 2⁶ 2 shifts + 1 mul + 1 add 4 SENS = C1 × 2¹⁶ + (C3 × dT) / 2⁷ 2 shifts + 1 mul + 1 add 4 P = (D1 × SENS / 2²¹ − OFF) / 2¹⁵ 1 mul + 2 shifts + 1 sub 4 Second-order correction (T < 20 °C) ~6 ops (conditional) 0–6 Compensation subtotal 17–23

getAltitude() — ISA barometric formula:

alt = 44330 × (1 − (P / 101325)^0.190295)
Operation NOP Division P / 101325 1 pow(x, 0.190295) — float power (log + mul + exp internally) ~20 1 − result + × 44330 2 Altitude subtotal ~23

NOP(readDigitalValue) ≈ 16 + 20 = 36 NOP(getAltitude) ≈ 23 NOP(AltimeterModule::read total) = 10 + 36 + 23 = 69

Memory:

Location Content Size Stack 3 temporary floats (T, P, alt) 12 B Output (g_adcs.altimeter) 3 × float + bool 13 B

docs.title: SensorManager::initAll / readAll
docs.text:
Linear loop over registered modules (max MAX_MODULES = 4):

Time complexity: O(N), N ≤ 4 Memory: O(1) — pointer array = 16 B (4 × 4 B)

docs.title: Summary — One complete loop() cycle
docs.text:
Algorithm NOP firmware NOP library NOP total Auxiliary memory IMU read (getEvent + copy) 18 76 94 37 B output + ~128 B stack Altimeter read (readDigitalValue + compensation + altitude) 10 59 69 13 B output + 12 B stack Pt1000 scan (×8, LUT included) 536 — 536 800 B flash + 41 B output Watchdog (CLK + I2C poll + sendStatus + encoding) 26 — 26 1 B stack SensorManager overhead 16 — 16 16 B Total per cycle 606 135 741 ~850 B flash · ~210 B stack

The dominant firmware cost is the 8-channel Pt1000 scan (~536 ops, driven by 8 × lutLookup). The dominant library cost is getEvent() (~76 ops), mainly I2C byte transfers and float scaling. pow() inside getAltitude() is the most expensive single call (~20 ops) outside the LUT scan.

docs.title: SAFARI ADCS — Architecture V2
docs.text: Project: Satellite Attitude & Formation — RP2040 Attitude Determination Board: Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040) Framework: Arduino-Mbed (PlatformIO, platform = raspberrypi) Revision: V2.1 — 2026-06-10

docs.title: 2.1 Full Table
docs.text: GPIO Protocol Connected to Signal Subsystem GPIO0 GPIO Mux — pin A0 Pt1000 select Pt1000 / Mux GPIO1 GPIO Mux — pin A1 Pt1000 select Pt1000 / Mux GPIO2 GPIO Mux — pin A2 Pt1000 select Pt1000 / Mux GPIO3 GPIO Switch ON/OFF Pt1000 Pt1000 / Mux GPIO4 I2C0 (SDA) Watchdog SDA Output ADCS Com ext GPIO5 I2C0 (SCL) Watchdog SCL Output ADCS Com ext GPIO6 I2C1 (SDA) IMU — SDA pin SDA IMU GPIO7 I2C1 (SCL) IMU — SCL pin SCL IMU GPIO8 SPI (MISO) Altimeter — SDO pin SDO (Alt → MCU) Altimeter GPIO9 SPI (CS) Altimeter — CSB pin CSB / CS Altimeter GPIO10 SPI (SCLK) Altimeter — SCLK pin SCLK Altimeter GPIO11 SPI (MOSI) Altimeter — SDI pin SDI Altimeter GPIO12 GPIO IMU — AD0/SDO pin AD0 : I2C address select (LOW → 0x68) IMU GPIO13 GPIO (IRQ) — not wired IMU INT1 — data-ready interrupt (potential use) IMU GPIO14 GPIO — not wired IMU FSYNC — accel frame sync (potential use) IMU GPIO15 — spare unassigned — GPIO16 — spare unassigned — GPIO17 — spare unassigned — GPIO18 — spare unassigned — GPIO19 GPIO (CLK) Watchdog CLK ext output ADCS Com ext GPIO26 ADC0 Op-amp output Pt1000 measure Pt1000 / Mux RUN GPIO (RST) USB connector External reset USB Communication

docs.title: 3.1 External Communication — Watchdog
docs.text:
Watchdog module
    ├── GPIO4  ←→ SDA (I2C0 — address 0x55)
    ├── GPIO5  ←→ SCL (I2C0)
    └── GPIO19 ──► CLK output (500 µs half-period, 1 kHz)
  • GPIO4/5: I2C0 — ADCS communicates with the external watchdog over this bus (Wire, default Mbed pins for Pico)
  • GPIO19: clock output pulsed once per read() cycle; idles LOW
  • ADCS → Watchdog: one status byte per loop cycle via sendStatus():
  • Bit 0 (WD_STATUS_IMU_OK): g_adcs.imu.valid
  • Bit 1 (WD_STATUS_ALT_OK): g_adcs.altimeter.valid
  • Bit 2 (WD_STATUS_PT_OK): g_adcs.pt1000.active_count > 0
  • Bits 3–7: reserved
  • RUN: hardware reset of the RP2040 from an external source

docs.title: 3.2 Pt1000 — Analog Multiplexer
docs.text:
Sensor: PTFC102B1G0 — TE Connectivity (PTF family)

Parameter Value Nominal resistance R₀ 1000 Ω at 0 °C Temperature coefficient 3850 ppm/K (α₀…₁₀₀) Tolerance (Class B) ±(0.30 + 0.005 × |T|) °C Operating range −50 °C to +600 °C

Architecture: MCU → MUX (8:1) → Pt1000 (1 of 8) → Op-amp → MCU (ADC)

RP2040
  │
  ├── GPIO0 (A0) ─┐
  ├── GPIO1 (A1) ─┼──► MUX 8:1 ──► Selected Pt1000 (S1 … S8)
  ├── GPIO2 (A2) ─┘         │
  │                         └──► Op-amp ──► GPIO26 (ADC0)
  └── GPIO3 ────────────────────► Switch ON/OFF (all Pt1000s)

Selection logic (A2:A1:A0):

A2 A1 A0 Selected Pt1000 0 0 0 S1 0 0 1 S2 0 1 0 S3 0 1 1 S4 1 0 0 S5 1 0 1 S6 1 1 0 S7 1 1 1 S8
  • GPIO3 powers all Pt1000s OFF (LOW) between measurements — saves power
  • GPIO26 (ADC0) samples the op-amp output; ADC resolution fixed to 10 bits (analogReadResolution(10))
  • MUX settling delay: 500 µs after each channel switch before sampling (to be refined after hardware characterization)

Voltage → temperature conversion — calibration LUT:

Source file: Vout_vs_T_20260603_180610.csv — 200 measured points, steady-state op-amp output at GPIO26 as a function of temperature.

LUT_V[200]   float array, −0.0145 V … 2.3058 V
T_CAL_MIN  = −60 °C
T_CAL_MAX  = +80 °C
T_CAL_STEP = (80 − (−60)) / 199 ≈ 0.7035 °C/step

Lookup: binary-search floor (no interpolation). v → lo such that LUT_V[lo] ≤ v < LUT_V[lo+1] → T = T_CAL_MIN + lo × T_CAL_STEP

Readings outside [LUT_V[0], LUT_V[199]] are rejected (valid[ch] = false).

docs.title: 3.3 Altimeter — MS5607-02BA03-50 (TE Connectivity)
docs.text:
Hardware target: SPI on GPIO8–11 (see GPIO map).

Current firmware implementation: I2C, via the bundled MS5607 library (Libnexamples/MS5607-master/). The available library uses Wire internally; the SPI pins (GPIO8–11) are reserved on the PCB but not driven by the current firmware. A SPI-based driver can be substituted in altimeter.h/.cpp when available without any other change.

RP2040 (hardware target)       MS5607
  │
  ├── GPIO10 (SCLK) ─────────►│ SCLK
  ├── GPIO11 (SDI / MOSI) ───►│ SDI
  ├── GPIO8  (SDO / MISO) ◄───│ SDO
  └── GPIO9  (CSB)  ─────────►│ CS

Initialization: _sensor.begin() — returns false if sensor not found.

Measurement cycle: 1. _sensor.readDigitalValue() → triggers ADC conversion; returns false on failure 2. _sensor.getTemperature() → T (°C) 3. _sensor.getPressure() → P (hPa) 4. _sensor.getAltitude() → altitude (m)

Validation ranges (firmware-enforced): - Pressure: 10 – 1200 hPa - Temperature: −40 °C to +85 °C

Values outside these ranges set valid = false and increment the failure counter.

docs.title: 3.4 IMU — ICM-20948 (Adafruit #4554)
docs.text:
9-axis IMU: accelerometer + gyroscope (ICM-20948) + integrated AK09916 magnetometer. I2C1 bus at 400 kHz, GPIO6/7.

Implementation note: Wire1 is not exported by the Arduino-Mbed variant for the Pico. A custom instance is created in imu.cpp: static TwoWire _wire1(PIN_IMU_SDA, PIN_IMU_SCL);

RP2040                         ICM-20948
  │
  ├── GPIO6  (SDA) ◄──────────►│ SDA
  ├── GPIO7  (SCL) ────────────►│ SCL
  └── GPIO12 (AD0) ────────────►│ AD0   (LOW → I2C address 0x68)

Initialization: 1. AD0 (GPIO12) driven LOW → address locked to 0x68, never changes at runtime 2. _wire1.begin() + _wire1.setClock(400000UL) 3. _icm.begin_I2C(0x68, &_wire1) — returns false if not found 4. Range configuration: - Accelerometer: ±4 g (ICM20948_ACCEL_RANGE_4_G) - Gyroscope: ±500 dps (ICM20948_GYRO_RANGE_500_DPS) - Magnetometer data rate: 10 Hz (AK09916_MAG_DATARATE_10_HZ)

Measurement cycle: 1. _icm.getEvent(&accel, &gyro, &temp, &mag) → fills sensor events 2. accel.acceleration.x/y/z → m/s² 3. gyro.gyro.x/y/z → rad/s 4. mag.magnetic.x/y/z → µT

Read validation: if the acceleration magnitude squared equals zero, the read is treated as failed (DATA_INVALID). Three consecutive failures trigger a watchdog status notification.

Attitude representation — unit quaternion q = [w, x, y, z]:

Source Role in fusion Gyroscope (°/s) Integrates angular rate → propagates quaternion at high frequency Accelerometer (g) Corrects pitch and roll at low frequency (gravity reference) Magnetometer (µT) Corrects yaw (Earth magnetic field reference)

Fusion algorithm (complementary filter, EKF, Madgwick…) to be selected after sensor characterization.

docs.title: 4.1 Principle
docs.text:
Each sensor is a fully independent module. The absence of a sensor never causes a crash or a hang. The firmware detects each sensor at initialization and silently disables absent modules.

main.cpp
  │
  ├── SensorManager        ← orchestrates modules, holds no sensor logic
  │     ├── ImuModule      ← optional
  │     ├── AltimeterModule← optional
  │     ├── Pt1000Module   ← optional
  │     └── WatchdogComm   ← optional
  │
  └── AdcsData g_adcs      ← shared data bus (each module writes only its section)

docs.title: 4.2 Common Interface (SensorModule)
docs.text:
Each module implements the same minimal interface:

class SensorModule {
public:
    virtual bool        init()            = 0;  // false if sensor absent/faulty
    virtual bool        read()            = 0;  // false if read fails
    virtual bool        isReady()   const = 0;
    virtual const char* name()      const = 0;
    virtual SensorError lastError() const = 0;
    virtual ~SensorModule() {}
};

Rules: - init() never hangs if the sensor does not respond - read() never blocks; returns false if data unavailable - No module calls another module directly

docs.title: 4.2.1 Error Codes
docs.text:
enum class SensorError : uint8_t {
    OK           = 0,
    NOT_FOUND,       // no response on bus (I2C NACK, SPI timeout)
    BAD_ID,          // unexpected WHO_AM_I or invalid PROM CRC
    DATA_INVALID,    // value outside physical range
    READ_FAIL        // partial or corrupted read
};

docs.title: 4.2.2 Recovery Policy
docs.text:
Event Immediate reaction After 3 consecutive failures init() → false module marked not-ready, Serial log none — module stays disabled read() → false data ignored, valid = false WatchdogComm::sendStatus() flags the failed sensor Locked I2C bus bus reset (Wire.end() / Wire.begin()) module permanently disabled

External watchdog: WatchdogComm is the ADCS-side interface to the independent watchdog module. It communicates over I2C0 (GPIO4/5) and sends a CLK pulse (GPIO19) every loop cycle. The communication protocol is defined on the watchdog side.

docs.title: 4.3 Sensor Manager (SensorManager)
docs.text:
class SensorManager {
    SensorModule* modules[MAX_MODULES];   // MAX_MODULES = 4
    uint8_t       count;
public:
    void registerModule(SensorModule* m);
    void initAll();   // calls init() on each; logs absent modules
    void readAll();   // skips modules that are not ready
};
  • initAll() sets isReady = false for any module whose init() returns false
  • readAll() silently skips non-ready modules
  • main.cpp selects which modules to register via #define ENABLE_*

docs.title: 4.4 Shared Data Bus (AdcsData g_adcs)
docs.text:
Each module writes only its own section; main.cpp reads all sections.

struct AdcsData {
    struct {
        float ax, ay, az;       // m/s²
        float gx, gy, gz;       // rad/s
        float mx, my, mz;       // µT
        bool  valid;
    } imu;

    struct {
        float pressure;         // hPa
        float temperature;      // °C
        float altitude;         // m
        bool  valid;
    } altimeter;

    struct {
        float   temps[8];       // °C, index 0..7 = S1..S8
        bool    valid[8];
        uint8_t active_count;
    } pt1000;

    struct {
        bool connected;
    } watchdog;
};

extern AdcsData g_adcs;

docs.title: 4.5 Compile-time Configuration (config.h)
docs.text:
#define ENABLE_IMU        1   // set to 0 to exclude module entirely
#define ENABLE_ALTIMETER  1
#define ENABLE_PT1000     1
#define ENABLE_WATCHDOG   1

#define PT1000_COUNT      8   // number of Pt1000 sensors wired (1–8)
#define MAX_MODULES       4

docs.title: 5. Execution Flow
docs.text:
setup()
  ├── Serial.begin(115200)          — waits up to 3 s for USB host, then proceeds
  └── SensorManager::initAll()
        ├── ImuModule::init()        → AD0 LOW, _wire1.begin(), probe 0x68
        ├── AltimeterModule::init()  → _sensor.begin() (I2C, library-internal)
        ├── Pt1000Module::init()     → GPIO0-3 config, ADC smoke-test on GPIO26
        └── WatchdogComm::init()     → GPIO19 LOW, Wire.begin(), probe 0x55

loop()  [period = 1000 ms, paced via millis()]
  └── SensorManager::readAll()
        ├── ImuModule::read()        → getEvent() → g_adcs.imu
        ├── AltimeterModule::read()  → readDigitalValue() → g_adcs.altimeter
        ├── Pt1000Module::read()     → MUX scan × 8, 500 µs settle → g_adcs.pt1000
        └── WatchdogComm::read()     → tickClock() + I2C poll → g_adcs.watchdog
  │
  ├── WatchdogComm::sendStatus(byte)  — IMU/Alt/Pt1000 status bits → watchdog
  └── printAdcsStatus()               — Serial print of g_adcs

docs.title: 6. Inter-module Dependencies
docs.text:
No direct dependencies between sensor modules.

Module       Depends on
───────────  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ImuModule    GPIO6, GPIO7, GPIO12  (I2C1 — custom TwoWire instance)
Altimeter    MS5607 library (I2C internal)  [GPIO8-11 reserved / unused]
Pt1000       GPIO0, GPIO1, GPIO2, GPIO3, GPIO26  (ADC0)
WatchdogComm GPIO4, GPIO5 (I2C0 — Wire default), GPIO19
main         SensorManager, AdcsData (g_adcs)

If a GPIO is absent or short-circuited, only the corresponding module is marked not ready. All other modules continue normally.

docs.title: 7.1 External Libraries — PlatformIO (lib_deps)
docs.text: Library Version Role Adafruit ICM20X ^2.0.5 ICM-20948 driver (IMU) Adafruit BusIO — I2C/SPI abstraction layer (Adafruit dependency) Adafruit Unified Sensor — Sensor event abstraction (Adafruit dependency) MS5607 (bundled) — MS5607 altimeter driver — I2C, from Libnexamples/MS5607-master/

docs.title: 7.2 Framework and SDK
docs.text: Component Usage Arduino.h Arduino core (timers, GPIO, ADC) Wire.h I2C0 (Wire — Watchdog) and I2C1 (TwoWire — IMU) SPI.h Reserved for future SPI altimeter driver

docs.title: 7.3 Internal Modules
docs.text: Module Files Depends on config include/config.h — sensors include/sensors.h, src/sensors.cpp defines SensorModule, AdcsData, SensorManager imu include/imu.h, src/imu.cpp sensors.h, config.h, Wire.h, Adafruit ICM20X altimeter include/altimeter.h, src/altimeter.cpp sensors.h, config.h, MS5607.h pt1000 include/pt1000.h, src/pt1000.cpp sensors.h, config.h, Arduino.h watchdog include/watchdog.h, src/watchdog.cpp sensors.h, config.h, Wire.h printStatus include/printStatus.h, src/printStatus.cpp sensors.h, imu.h, altimeter.h, pt1000.h

docs.text:
main.cpp
  ├── Arduino.h
  ├── Wire.h
  ├── config.h
  ├── sensors.h / sensors.cpp
  │
  ├── imu.h / imu.cpp
  │     ├── sensors.h

docs.title: 7.5 Embedded Algorithms (no external library)
docs.text: Algorithm Module Notes LUT binary-search floor lookup pt1000 200-entry LUT_V[], lo + (hi-lo)/2 avoids uint16_t overflow Watchdog status byte encoding main / watchdog 3-bit field: IMU OK, Alt OK, Pt1000 active IMU sensor fusion → quaternion imu Algorithm TBD after characterization (Madgwick / Mahony / complementary) Digital filters all modules To be added after real sensor testing

docs.title: SAFARI ADCS Borealis V1.2 — Test Report
docs.text: Platform: Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040) Framework: Arduino-Mbed (platform = raspberrypi, framework = arduino) Toolchain: PlatformIO + Unity (bundled inside libmbed.a) Hardware available: Pico only — no sensors connected (ICM-20948, MS5607, Pt1000, Watchdog absent) Test environment: pico_test (see platformio.ini)

docs.title: Tier 1 — No extra hardware (bare Pico)
docs.text: Suite Tests Result test_pt1000_lut 5 PASSED test_gpio_mux 3 PASSED test_gpio_watchdog 2 PASSED test_gpio_imu 2 PASSED test_adc_pt1000 2 PASSED test_sim_sensor_manager 5 PASSED Subtotal 19 19 / 19 PASSED

docs.title: Tier 2 — Wire simulation (bare wire between Pico header pins)
docs.text: Suite Sensor Wires required Tests Result test_pt1000_sim_gpio Pt1000 ADC pin 20 → pin 31 4 not yet run test_sim_spi_altimeter MS5607 SPI pin 15 → pin 11 4 not yet run test_sim_i2c_imu ICM-20948 I2C1 pin 20 → pin 9 4 not yet run test_sim_i2c_watchdog Watchdog I2C0+CLK pin 20→pin 6, pin 25→pin 21 5 not yet run Subtotal 17 pending

docs.title: Environment Configuration
docs.text:
[env:pico_test]
platform        = raspberrypi
board           = pico
framework       = arduino
lib_deps =
    adafruit/Adafruit ICM20X@^2.0.5
    adafruit/Adafruit BusIO
    adafruit/Adafruit Unified Sensor
lib_extra_dirs  = Libnexamples
test_build_src  = no
monitor_speed   = 115200
build_flags     = -Wl,--allow-multiple-definition

Two flags are mandatory and non-obvious:

  • test_build_src = no — prevents src/main.cpp's setup()/loop() from being compiled during test builds, which would create duplicate entry-point symbols with the Unity runner.
  • -Wl,--allow-multiple-definition — the Arduino-Mbed framework bundles unity.o inside libmbed.a; PlatformIO also links its own libUnity.a. Without this flag, the linker aborts with multiple definition of 'UnityDefaultTestRun'. The flag keeps the first definition and discards the duplicate.

docs.title: 1. test/test_pt1000_lut/ — LUT Binary-Search Logic
docs.text:
Validates the Pt1000 calibration lookup table without any GPIO or peripheral use. The 200-entry LUT_V[] array and lutLookup() function are copied locally from pt1000.cpp so the test is fully self-contained.

Calibration constants: Range −60 °C → +80 °C, 200 entries, step ≈ 0.7035 °C/entry.

Test What it checks test_lut_lower_bound lutLookup(LUT_V[0]) returns −60.0 °C ± 0.01 test_lut_upper_bound lutLookup(LUT_V[199]) returns index-198 temperature (floor semantics) test_lut_midpoint lutLookup(LUT_V[99]) returns exact mid-table temperature test_lut_monotone 10 sampled adjacent pairs: T(v_i) ≤ T(v_{i+1}) — no inversion test_lut_step_size T(LUT_V[50]) − T(LUT_V[49]) equals T_CAL_STEP ± 0.01

Result: 5 / 5 PASSED

docs.title: 2. test/test_gpio_mux/ — MUX Address and Power Pins
docs.text:
Verifies GPIO output drive and readback for the 8:1 analog MUX (CD4051 or equivalent) and the Pt1000 power switch. No sensor hardware required.

Pins under test: GPIO0 (A0), GPIO1 (A1), GPIO2 (A2), GPIO3 (PT1000_SW)

Test What it checks test_mux_pins_toggle Each of GPIO0–2: digitalWrite(HIGH) → digitalRead() == HIGH, then LOW/LOW test_pt1000_switch_toggle GPIO3: HIGH/LOW drive and readback test_channel_select_all Encodes channels 0–7 as A2:A1:A0 bit pattern; reads back all 3 pins per channel

Result: 3 / 3 PASSED

docs.title: 3. test/test_gpio_watchdog/ — Watchdog CLK Output
docs.text:
Verifies the Watchdog clock output pin. The I2C0 bus scan (GPIO4/5) is intentionally excluded — see Known Limitations below.

Pin under test: GPIO19 (WD_CLK)

Test What it checks test_clk_toggle digitalWrite(HIGH) → readback HIGH, then LOW → readback LOW test_clk_pulse Full 50% duty-cycle pulse at WD_CLK_HALF_US timing; asserts state at each half-cycle

Result: 2 / 2 PASSED

docs.title: 4. test/test_gpio_imu/ — IMU Address Pin and I2C1 Bus
docs.text:
Verifies the AD0 pin that locks the ICM-20948 I2C address and probes I2C1 (GPIO6/7). Uses static TwoWire _wire1(PIN_IMU_SDA, PIN_IMU_SCL) — Wire1 is not exported by the Arduino-Mbed variant for the Pico, so the custom instance is mandatory (mirrors imu.cpp).

Pins under test: GPIO12 (IMU_AD0), GPIO6/7 (I2C1 SDA/SCL)

Test What it checks test_ad0_low GPIO12 driven LOW, digitalRead() confirms LOW — locks address to 0x68 test_i2c1_scan Probes 0x68 on I2C1; non-fatal — prints result to Serial, no assertion

Note: test_ad0_high (address 0x69) was removed — AD0 HIGH is never used in the firmware. The ICM-20948 address is permanently locked to 0x68.

Result: 2 / 2 PASSED

docs.title: 5. test/test_adc_pt1000/ — ADC GPIO26 and MUX Switching
docs.text:
Verifies that the ADC peripheral and MUX switching work correctly. A floating pin still returns a value in [0, 1023] — sensor connection is not required.

Pins under test: GPIO26 (ADC), GPIO0–2 (MUX), GPIO3 (SW)

Test What it checks test_adc_channel0_range Powers the circuit, reads channel 0, powers off; result ∈ [0, 1023] test_adc_all_channels Loops channels 0–7: selects via MUX, reads ADC; each result ∈ [0, 1023]

Result: 2 / 2 PASSED

docs.title: 6. test/test_sim_sensor_manager/ — SensorManager Simulation
docs.text:
Tests SensorManager orchestration and g_adcs shared data bus using stub modules. No peripheral code is called — stubs inject hardcoded data into g_adcs as real modules would. Includes ../src/sensors.cpp directly (required because test_build_src = no).

Stub modules: FakeImuModule, FakeAltimeterModule, FakePt1000Module, FakeWatchdogModule, AbsentModule (always fails init()).

Test Scenario test_sim_all_modules_present 4 stubs init successfully; readAll() populates g_adcs without crash test_sim_imu_data_values Verifies g_adcs.imu.az ≈ 9.81, mx ≈ 25.0, mz ≈ −42.0, valid == true test_sim_pt1000_all_channels active_count == 8; all 8 valid[i] == true; temps[i] ≈ 20 + i °C test_sim_absent_module_skipped AbsentModule + FakeImuModule: absent init fails, IMU still reads; no crash test_sim_partial_failure 2 present + 2 absent: readAll() skips absent modules silently

setUp() resets g_adcs = {} before each test to prevent state leaking between cases.

Result: 5 / 5 PASSED

docs.title: Tier 2 — Wire Simulation Suites
docs.text: These suites require one or two bare wires between Pico header pins. They test the full acquisition chain of each sensor interface with no actual sensor present.

docs.title: 7. test/test_pt1000_sim_gpio/ — Pt1000 Full Chain (ADC)
docs.text:
Wire: Pico pin 20 (GP15 / stimulus) → Pico pin 31 (GP26 / ADC0)

GP15 drives 3.3 V (HIGH) or 0 V (LOW) directly onto the ADC input, simulating the Pt1000 voltage divider output at the two extremes of the calibration range.

Test Stimulus Expected test_sim_wire_connected HIGH then LOW on GP15 delta ≥ 800 LSB between the two reads test_sim_high_stimulus GP15 HIGH (3.3 V) raw > 900 and LUT → T > 70 °C test_sim_low_stimulus GP15 LOW (0 V) raw < 50 and LUT → T < −50 °C test_sim_adc_stability GP15 HIGH, 2 reads |r1 − r2| ≤ 5 LSB

docs.title: 8. test/test_sim_spi_altimeter/ — MS5607 Altimeter SPI Bus
docs.text:
Wire: Pico pin 15 (GP11 / MOSI) → Pico pin 11 (GP8 / MISO)

MOSI is looped back to MISO: every byte clocked out appears immediately on the input. CS (GP9), SCK (GP10) and MOSI (GP11) are driven by the SPI1 peripheral.

Test What it checks test_sim_spi_wire_connected tx=0x00 → rx=0x00; if MISO floats it returns 0xFF test_sim_spi_loopback_byte tx=0xAA → rx=0xAA test_sim_spi_loopback_pattern 6-byte burst [0x00, 0xFF, 0x5A, 0xA5, 0x01, 0xFE] each echoed back test_sim_spi_cs_timing CS HIGH at idle → LOW on select → HIGH after deselect

docs.title: 9. test/test_sim_i2c_imu/ — ICM-20948 IMU I2C1 SDA Line
docs.text:
Wire: Pico pin 20 (GP15 / stimulus) → Pico pin 9 (GP6 / I2C1 SDA)

Wire.begin() is NOT called — GP6 is used as plain INPUT. GP15 drives the SDA line as an external agent would (sensor ACK, data bits).

Test Stimulus Expected test_sim_sda_wire_connected HIGH then LOW GP6 follows: 1 then 0 test_sim_sda_driven_low GP15 LOW GP6 reads LOW test_sim_sda_driven_high GP15 HIGH GP6 reads HIGH test_sim_sda_toggle Alternate 5× GP6 matches each level

SCL (GP7 / pin 10) can be tested identically with a second wire to any free output pin.

docs.title: 10. test/test_sim_i2c_watchdog/ — Watchdog I2C0 SDA + CLK
docs.text:
Wire A (SDA): Pico pin 20 (GP15) → Pico pin 6 (GP4 / I2C0 SDA) Wire B (CLK): Pico pin 25 (GP19 / WD_CLK) → Pico pin 21 (GP16 / monitor)

Wire.begin() (I2C0) is NEVER called — avoids the Mbed blocking bug (see Known Limitations). SDA tests use Wire A only. CLK external-readback tests use Wire B only.

Test Wire What it checks test_sim_sda_wire_connected A HIGH/LOW delta on GP4 confirms wire present test_sim_sda_driven_low A GP4 follows GP15 LOW test_sim_sda_driven_high A GP4 follows GP15 HIGH test_sim_clk_external_high B GP16 reads HIGH while GP19 is HIGH test_sim_clk_pulse_external B GP16 tracks each half-cycle of a CLK pulse

docs.title: How to Run
docs.text:
# Tier 1 — bare Pico, no wires
pio test -e pico_test -f test_pt1000_lut
pio test -e pico_test -f test_gpio_mux
pio test -e pico_test -f test_gpio_watchdog
pio test -e pico_test -f test_gpio_imu
pio test -e pico_test -f test_adc_pt1000
pio test -e pico_test -f test_sim_sensor_manager

# Tier 2 — wire simulations (add wires before each run)
pio test -e pico_test -f test_pt1000_sim_gpio      # wire: pin20 → pin31
pio test -e pico_test -f test_sim_spi_altimeter    # wire: pin15 → pin11
pio test -e pico_test -f test_sim_i2c_imu          # wire: pin20 → pin9
pio test -e pico_test -f test_sim_i2c_watchdog     # wires: pin20→pin6, pin25→pin21

# All suites at once (Tier 1 only — remove wires or run Tier 2 separately)
pio test -e pico_test

# Serial monitor
pio device monitor --baud 115200

docs.title: I2C0 scan excluded from test_gpio_watchdog
docs.text:
The Mbed I2C driver used by the default Wire object has no timeout on Wire.endTransmission(). When no device acknowledges on I2C0 (GPIO4/5), the call blocks indefinitely — observed hang of 20+ minutes in practice. The I2C0 bus scan was therefore removed from test_gpio_watchdog. CLK GPIO tests are sufficient to verify the Watchdog pin without triggering this bug.

I2C1 (_wire1 on GPIO6/7) does not exhibit this problem: endTransmission() returned normally in test_i2c1_scan even with no sensor present.

docs.title: I2C scans are non-fatal
docs.text: test_i2c1_scan probes address 0x68 and prints the result to Serial but makes no assertion. The test passes whether or not the ICM-20948 is physically connected. This is intentional — the suite is designed to run on bare hardware.

docs.title: Issues Resolved During Development
docs.text: Issue Root cause Fix 'ADC_VREF' redeclared RP2040 SDK defines PinName ADC_VREF in PinNames.h Renamed to PT1000_VREF in pt1000.cpp Wire.setSDA()/setSCL() missing Not available on MbedI2C class Removed calls; Wire uses GPIO4/5 by default Wire1 undeclared Not exported by Arduino-Mbed Pico variant Created static TwoWire _wire1(6, 7) manually TEST_ASSERT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL missing Not available in all Unity versions bundled by Mbed Replaced with TEST_ASSERT_TRUE_MESSAGE(raw >= 0, ...) TEST_MESSAGE() missing Not in bundled Unity Replaced with Serial.println() WATCHDOG_I2C_ADDR/WD_CLK_HALF_US undeclared Missing include Added #include "watchdog.h" Duplicate setup()/loop() link error src/main.cpp compiled during test builds Added test_build_src = no Multiple Unity symbol definitions libmbed.a bundles unity.o; PlatformIO adds libUnity.a Added -Wl,--allow-multiple-definition test_gpio_watchdog hung 20 min Wire.endTransmission() blocks on NACK (Mbed, no timeout) Removed I2C0 scan entirely from that suite Cascading upload failures on last 2 suites Pico USB lost after 20-min hang; serial port PermissionError Fixed by removing the blocking test

docs.title: Firmware Architecture — SAFARI ADCS V1.2
docs.text: Platform: Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040) · Arduino-Mbed · PlatformIO Revision: V2.1 — 2026-06-10

docs.title: 1. Overview
docs.text: The firmware is organized around a central SensorManager that orchestrates up to four independent sensor modules. Each module implements the same abstract interface (SensorModule). Results are written to a single shared data bus (AdcsData g_adcs). No module depends on another.

docs.title: 2. Class Hierarchy
docs.text:
@startuml
skinparam classAttributeIconSize 0
skinparam classFontSize 13

abstract class SensorModule {
    + {abstract} init()    : bool
    + {abstract} read()    : bool
    + {abstract} isReady() : bool
    + {abstract} name()    : const char*
    + {abstract} lastError(): SensorError
}

enum SensorError {
    OK
    NOT_FOUND
    BAD_ID
    DATA_INVALID
    READ_FAIL
}

class ImuModule {
    - _icm       : Adafruit_ICM20948
    - _wire1     : TwoWire
    - _ready     : bool
    - _failCount : uint8_t
    - _error     : SensorError
    + init()  : bool
    + read()  : bool
}

class AltimeterModule {
    - _sensor    : MS5607
    - _ready     : bool
    - _failCount : uint8_t
    - _error     : SensorError
    + init()  : bool
    + read()  : bool
}

class Pt1000Module {
    - _ready : bool
    - _error : SensorError
    + init()          : bool
    + read()          : bool
    - selectChannel(ch: uint8_t)
    - lutLookup(v: float) : float
}

class WatchdogComm {
    - _ready : bool
    - _error : SensorError
    + init()              : bool
    + read()              : bool
    + sendStatus(byte: uint8_t)
    - tickClock()
}

class SensorManager {
    - modules[MAX_MODULES] : SensorModule*
    - count                : uint8_t
    + registerModule(m: SensorModule*)
    + initAll()
    + readAll()
}

struct AdcsData {
    + imu        : {ax,ay,az · gx,gy,gz · mx,my,mz · valid}
    + altimeter  : {pressure · temperature · altitude · valid}
    + pt1000     : {temps[8] · valid[8] · active_count}
    + watchdog   : {connected}
}

SensorModule  <|--  ImuModule
SensorModule  <|--  AltimeterModule
SensorModule  <|--  Pt1000Module
SensorModule  <|--  WatchdogComm

SensorModule  ..>   SensorError
SensorManager  o--  SensorModule : "manages [0..4]"
SensorModule  ..>   AdcsData     : "writes own section"

note bottom of AdcsData : "extern AdcsData g_adcs\ndefined in sensors.cpp"
@enduml

docs.title: 3. Shared Data Bus — AdcsData
docs.text:
@startuml
skinparam objectAttributeIconSize 0

object "g_adcs : AdcsData" as g {
    imu.ax / ay / az    : float   ' m/s²
    imu.gx / gy / gz    : float   ' rad/s
    imu.mx / my / mz    : float   ' µT
    imu.valid           : bool
    ──────────────────────────────
    altimeter.pressure  : float   ' hPa
    altimeter.temperature: float  ' °C
    altimeter.altitude  : float   ' m
    altimeter.valid     : bool
    ──────────────────────────────
    pt1000.temps[8]     : float   ' °C
    pt1000.valid[8]     : bool
    pt1000.active_count : uint8_t
    ──────────────────────────────
    watchdog.connected  : bool
}
@enduml

Each module writes only its own section. main.cpp reads all sections after readAll().

docs.title: 4. Initialization Sequence — setup()
docs.text:
@startuml
participant "main.cpp"      as main
participant SensorManager   as mgr
participant ImuModule       as imu
participant AltimeterModule as alt
participant Pt1000Module    as pt
participant WatchdogComm    as wd

main -> main : Serial.begin(115200)\nwait USB ≤ 3 s

main -> mgr : registerModule(&imu)
main -> mgr : registerModule(&altimeter)
main -> mgr : registerModule(&pt1000)
main -> mgr : registerModule(&watchdog)

main -> mgr : initAll()

mgr -> imu  : init()
imu --> mgr : true / false

mgr -> alt  : init()
alt --> mgr : true / false

mgr -> pt   : init()
pt  --> mgr : true / false

mgr -> wd   : init()
wd  --> mgr : true / false

note right of mgr
  Modules returning false
  are marked not-ready.
  readAll() will skip them.
end note
@enduml

docs.title: 5. Main Loop Sequence — loop() (period = 1000 ms)
docs.text:
@startuml
participant "main.cpp"      as main
participant SensorManager   as mgr
participant ImuModule       as imu
participant AltimeterModule as alt
participant Pt1000Module    as pt
participant WatchdogComm    as wd
participant "g_adcs"        as data

loop every 1000 ms  [paced via millis()]

    main -> mgr : readAll()

    mgr -> imu : read()
    imu -> data : write imu section
    imu --> mgr : true / false

    mgr -> alt : read()
    alt -> data : write altimeter section
    alt --> mgr : true / false

    mgr -> pt  : read()
    pt  -> data : write pt1000 section
    pt  --> mgr : true / false

    mgr -> wd  : read()  [tickClock() + I2C poll]
    wd  -> data : write watchdog section
    wd  --> mgr : true / false

    main -> wd   : sendStatus(status_byte)
    note right : bits: IMU_OK | ALT_OK | PT_OK

    main -> main : printAdcsStatus()

end
@enduml

docs.title: 6. Recovery Policy
docs.text:
@startuml
start

:module.read();

if (returns false?) then (yes)
    :data ignored\nvalid = false;
    :failCount++;
    if (failCount >= 3?) then (yes)
        :WatchdogComm::sendStatus()\nflags faulty sensor;
    else (no)
    endif
else (no)
    :failCount = 0;
    :g_adcs updated;
endif

stop
@enduml
Event Immediate reaction After 3 failures init() → false module disabled, Serial log stays disabled read() → false valid = false watchdog notified via sendStatus() Locked I2C bus Wire.end() / Wire.begin() module permanently disabled

docs.title: Subsystem — Pt1000 Temperature Sensors
docs.text: Sensor: PTFC102B1G0 — TE Connectivity (PTF family, Class B) Channels: 8 × Pt1000, analog 8:1 MUX Files: include/pt1000.h · src/pt1000.cpp

docs.title: 1. Hardware Connections
docs.text:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle

node "Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040)" {
    component "GPIO0  (MUX A0)" as A0
    component "GPIO1  (MUX A1)" as A1
    component "GPIO2  (MUX A2)" as A2
    component "GPIO3  (SW)"     as SW
    component "GPIO26 (ADC0)"   as ADC
}

component "MUX 8:1\n(CD4051 or equiv.)" as MUX
component "Op-amp\n(voltage buffer)"     as AMP
component "S1…S8\nPt1000 (1000 Ω @ 0°C)" as PT

A0  --> MUX : A0
A1  --> MUX : A1
A2  --> MUX : A2
SW  --> MUX : EN / power switch

MUX --> PT  : selects one channel
PT  --> AMP : Vpt1000
AMP --> ADC : Vout  [0 … 3.3 V]
@enduml
GPIO Role State GPIO0–2 MUX address A2:A1:A0 OUTPUT GPIO3 Circuit power switch HIGH = ON, LOW = OFF GPIO26 ADC0 — op-amp output INPUT ANALOG

docs.title: 2. Class Diagram
docs.text:
@startuml
skinparam classAttributeIconSize 0

abstract class SensorModule

class Pt1000Module {
    - _ready : bool
    - _error : SensorError
    --
    + init()  : bool
    + read()  : bool
    + isReady()   : bool
    + name()      : const char*
    + lastError() : SensorError
    --
    - selectChannel(ch : uint8_t)
    - lutLookup(v : float) : float
}

note right of Pt1000Module
  Constants (static, file-scope):
  LUT_V[200]   : const float  (800 B flash)
  T_CAL_MIN    = −60 °C
  T_CAL_MAX    = +80 °C
  T_CAL_STEP   ≈  0.7035 °C/step
  PT1000_VREF  =  3.3 V
  ADC_MAX      =  1023
  MUX_SETTLE_US = 500 µs
end note

SensorModule <|-- Pt1000Module
@enduml

docs.title: 3. Initialization Sequence — init()
docs.text:
@startuml
participant "Pt1000Module" as pt
participant "Arduino GPIO" as gpio
participant "Arduino ADC"  as adc

pt -> gpio : pinMode(A0,A1,A2,SW, OUTPUT)
pt -> gpio : digitalWrite(SW, LOW)   ' circuit OFF
pt -> adc  : analogReadResolution(10)

note over pt : Smoke test — confirm ADC responds
pt -> gpio : selectChannel(0)
pt -> gpio : digitalWrite(SW, HIGH)  ' circuit ON
pt -> pt   : delay(5 ms)
pt -> adc  : analogRead(GPIO26) → raw  [discarded]
pt -> gpio : digitalWrite(SW, LOW)   ' circuit OFF

pt -> pt   : _ready = true\n_error = OK
pt -> pt   : Serial.println("[Pt1000] OK")
@enduml

docs.title: 4. Measurement Sequence — read() (8 channels)
docs.text:
@startuml
participant "Pt1000Module" as pt
participant "MUX (GPIO0-2)" as mux
participant "ADC (GPIO26)"  as adc
participant "g_adcs.pt1000" as data

pt -> pt  : digitalWrite(SW, HIGH)  ' power ON

loop ch = 0 to 7

    pt  -> mux : selectChannel(ch)\n  A0=(ch>>0)&1\n  A1=(ch>>1)&1\n  A2=(ch>>2)&1
    pt  -> pt  : delayMicroseconds(500)  ' RC settling

    pt  -> adc : analogRead(GPIO26) → raw
    pt  -> pt  : v = raw / 1023.0 × 3.3

    alt v in [LUT_V[0], LUT_V[199]]
        pt -> pt   : T = lutLookup(v)
        pt -> data : temps[ch] = T\nvalid[ch] = true\nactive_count++
    else out of range
        pt -> data : temps[ch] = 0.0\nvalid[ch] = false
    end

end

pt -> pt : digitalWrite(SW, LOW)  ' power OFF
@enduml

docs.title: 5. LUT Binary-Search Algorithm — lutLookup(v)
docs.text:
@startuml
start

:lo = 0
hi = 199;

while (hi − lo > 1?) is (yes)
    :mid = lo + (hi − lo) / 2;
    if (LUT_V[mid] ≤ v?) then (yes)
        :lo = mid;
    else (no)
        :hi = mid;
    endif
endwhile

:return T_CAL_MIN + lo × T_CAL_STEP;

stop

note right
  NOP ≈ 58 (7 iterations × 8 ops + 2)
  Resolution: ~0.7035 °C/step (floor)
  No interpolation applied
end note
@enduml

Complexity:

Metric Value Time O(log N) — 7 iterations for N = 200 NOP ≈ 58 ops per call Stack 10 B (lo, hi, mid, v) Flash 800 B (LUT_V[200])

docs.title: 7. Calibration LUT
docs.text:
Source file: Vout_vs_T_20260603_180610.csv 200 measured points of steady-state op-amp output voltage at GPIO26 as a function of temperature.

Parameter Value Range −60 °C → +80 °C Entries 200 Step ≈ 0.7035 °C/entry V min −0.0145 V (LUT_V[0]) V max 2.3058 V (LUT_V[199]) Strategy Floor (no interpolation)

docs.title: Subsystem — IMU ICM-20948
docs.text: Sensor: ICM-20948 — TDK InvenSense (Adafruit #4554) Axes: Accelerometer (3) + Gyroscope (3) + Magnetometer AK09916 (3) = 9-axis Bus: I2C1, 400 kHz, address 0x68 Files: include/imu.h · src/imu.cpp

docs.title: 1. Hardware Connections
docs.text:
@startuml
skinparam componentStyle rectangle

node "Raspberry Pi Pico (RP2040)" {
    component "GPIO6  (I2C1 SDA)" as SDA
    component "GPIO7  (I2C1 SCL)" as SCL
    component "GPIO12 (AD0)"      as AD0
}

component "ICM-20948\n(I2C addr 0x68)" as ICM {
    component "Accel\n±4 g" as ACC
    component "Gyro\n±500 dps" as GYR
    component "Mag AK09916\n10 Hz" as MAG
}

SDA <--> ICM : SDA
SCL  --> ICM : SCL
AD0  --> ICM : AD0 = LOW → addr 0x68

note bottom of AD0
  Held LOW at startup.
  Address locked to 0x68,
  never changes at runtime.
end note
@enduml
GPIO Role Direction GPIO6 I2C1 SDA bidirectional GPIO7 I2C1 SCL output (master) GPIO12 AD0 address select OUTPUT, always LOW

Implementation note: Wire1 is not exported by the Arduino-Mbed variant for the Pico. A dedicated instance is created: static TwoWire _wire1(PIN_IMU_SDA, PIN_IMU_SCL);

class "Adafruit_ICM20948" as ICM_LIB {
    + begin_I2C(addr, wire) : bool
    + setAccelRange(range)
    + setGyroRange(range)
    + setMagDataRate(rate)
    + getEvent(accel, gyro, temp, mag)
}

    + begin()
