docs.title: 7.6 Command Terminal (commandes au sol / banc)
docs.text:
Interpreteur de commandes non bloquant, lit caractere par caractere depuis un HardwareSerial (Serial USB au banc, ttc.stream() RFD900x en vol). Le dispatch se declenche sur \n ou \r. Appele via terminal.poll() dans loop().

Function File Status Priority Description CommandTerminal::init(port) CommandTerminal.cpp DONE -- Associe le terminal a un HardwareSerial& (USB ou TT&C) CommandTerminal::poll() CommandTerminal.cpp DONE HIGH Lecture non bloquante, accumule jusqu'a \n/\r puis dispatch() CommandTerminal::dispatch() CommandTerminal.cpp DONE -- Analyse la ligne et execute la commande Commande Effet help Liste les commandes status faults, mask, Core 0 vivant, uptime counters Compteur + etat par canal (OK / disable prolonge / DEFINITIF) reset <0-4> resetFaultCounter(ch) disable <0-4> markDisabled(ch) kick kick() manuel state Etat de vol courant + uptime reboot confirm fullSystemReset() (mot confirm obligatoire)

Securite : reboot sans confirm est traite comme une commande inconnue. Le buffer d'entree est borne (CMD_BUF_SIZE 64) : au-dela, les octets excess sont ignores sans debordement.

docs.title: 8.1 Requirement
docs.text:
All modules must not power up simultaneously to avoid inrush current spikes that could trip fuses or brown out the supply.

Each module is powered individually, followed by a stabilization delay and a communication test to confirm the module responds before moving to the next. On failure, the module is marked disabled and the sequence continues without it.

docs.title: 8.2 Power-Up Order
docs.text:
Step Module Stabilization Communication Test Test Timeout 1 TT&C 500 ms Send UART ping to RFD900x, wait ACK 2 s 2 IbNav 1000 ms Read NMEA bytes on I2C0 (addr IBNAV_I2C_ADDR) 3 s 3 ADCS 500 ms Wait for any byte on UART1 (raw text stream) 3 s 4 Jetson 2000 ms Ping I2C address JETSON_I2C_ADDR, wait Wire ACK 5 s 5 A7Sii 500 ms Check F1 HIGH (no TPS1H200 fault) 500 ms

TT&C powered first -- required to log boot results to ground. Jetson 2 s delay -- Linux boot time on embedded module.

docs.title: 8.3 Failure Behavior
docs.text:
Test passed  -> log "[BOOT] <module> OK" -> continue sequence
Test failed  -> log "[BOOT] <module> FAIL -- skipped"
             -> module marked DISABLED in watchdog _disabledMask
             -> watchdog configured to skip this module
             -> TT&C alerts ground

A module that fails the boot test can be retried via TT&C ground command.

docs.title: 9.1 Overview
docs.text:
The RP2040 OBC collects data from all subsystems, builds a structured packet, and transmits it via RFD900x (TT&C) to ground.

[IbNav GPS]  --I2C0 (GPIO 0/1)----->
[ADCS]       --UART1 (GPIO 8/9)---->  [RP2040 OBC]  --> Packet Builder --> [RFD900x TT&C] --> Ground
  +- IMU                                                  (UART0, GPIO 28/29)
  +- Baro
  +- 8x Temp
[Jetson]     --I2C1 (Wire1, 14/15)>

Allocation des peripheriques RP2040 : I2C0 = IbNav, I2C1 = Jetson, UART0 = TT&C (RFD900x), UART1 = ADCS. Les 2 I2C et 2 UART sont distincts.

docs.title: REQ-DAQ-01 -- IbNav GPS (I2C0, GPIO 0/1)
docs.text:
Field Format Rate Latitude float, decimal degrees 1 Hz Longitude float, decimal degrees 1 Hz Altitude (MSL) float, metres 1 Hz Ground speed float, m/s 1 Hz Heading float, degrees 1 Hz Fix quality uint8 (0=none, 1=GPS, 2=DGPS) 1 Hz Satellites uint8 1 Hz PPS health bool (pulse present < 1100ms) continuous

Protocol: NMEA 0183 (GGA, RMC sentences) lu via le bus I2C/DDC du module. Adresse I2C : IBNAV_I2C_ADDR (TODO a confirmer). 1PPS materiel sur GPIO 4.

docs.title: REQ-DAQ-02 -- ADCS (UART1, GPIO 8 TX / 9 RX)
docs.text:
The ADCS has its own processor that aggregates IMU, barometer, and 8 temperature probes, then streams a structured UART packet to the RP2040.

Field Format Rate Accelerometer X/Y/Z float, m/s^2 10 Hz Gyroscope X/Y/Z float, deg/s 10 Hz Roll / Pitch / Yaw float, degrees 10 Hz Absolute pressure float, hPa 1 Hz Barometric altitude float, metres 1 Hz Temp[0..7] float x8, degC 0.5 Hz

TEMPORARY FORMAT -- to be replaced per protocol agreed with ADCS team. Baud rate: 115200. Liaison UART en texte brut (raw), pas de paquet binaire. Pas de sync bytes : sur une liaison UART point a point, les octets sont lus directement via ADCS::forwardRaw(). Le parsing en champs viendra avec collectADCS() une fois le format texte confirme. GPIO 7 (CLK_ADCS) used as sync/interrupt signal from ADCS. GPIO 10 (RUN_ADCS): start/stop command for ADCS processor.

docs.title: REQ-DAQ-05 -- Jetson (I2C1 -- Wire1, GPIO 14/15)
docs.text:
Field Format Rate Operational status uint8 (enum) 1 Hz CPU load uint8, % 1 Hz GPU/CPU temperature float, degC 1 Hz Science data (summary) bytes[N] on event

Dedicated Wire1 bus (I2C1) -- isolated from ADCS bus to avoid conflicts. GPIO 13 (CLK_Jetson) used as sync/interrupt/heartbeat from Jetson. Heartbeat absence > 5s -> watchdog reacts. I2C address to confirm with Jetson firmware.

docs.title: REQ-DAQ-06 -- OBC Health (RP2040 internal)
docs.text: Field Format Rate Flight state uint8 (enum) 1 Hz Fault flags (5 bits) uint8 bitmask 1 Hz Uptime uint32, seconds 1 Hz Battery voltage float, V (ADC) 0.5 Hz RP2040 temperature float, degC (internal ADC) 0.5 Hz

docs.title: 9.3 Telemetry Packet Structure
docs.text:
Offset  Size   Field
--------------------------------------------------
0       2B     Sync header         0xAA 0x55
2       1B     Packet version      0x01
3       1B     FlightState         enum uint8
4       4B     Uptime              uint32 seconds
8       4B     Latitude            float             (offset  8 % 4 = 0 [x])
12      4B     Longitude           float
16      4B     Altitude GPS        float metres
20      4B     Ground speed        float m/s
24      4B     Heading             float degrees
28      1B     GPS fix quality     uint8
29      1B     GPS satellites      uint8
30      2B     _pad0               alignment padding
32      4B     Baro altitude       float metres      (offset 32 % 4 = 0 [x])
36      4B     Pressure            float hPa
40      4B     Accel X             float m/s^2
44      4B     Accel Y             float m/s^2
48      4B     Accel Z             float m/s^2
52      4B     Gyro X              float deg/s
56      4B     Gyro Y              float deg/s
60      4B     Gyro Z              float deg/s
64      4B     Roll                float deg
68      4B     Pitch               float deg
72      4B     Yaw                 float deg
76      32B    Temp[0..7]          float x8 degC       (offset 76 % 4 = 0 [x])
108     1B     Jetson status       uint8
109     1B     Jetson CPU %        uint8
110     2B     _pad1               alignment padding
112     4B     Jetson temp         float degC          (offset 112 % 4 = 0 [x])
116     4B     Battery voltage     float V           (offset 116 % 4 = 0 [x])
120     1B     Fault flags         bitmask
121     1B     _pad2               alignment padding
122     2B     CRC-16              CCITT
--------------------------------------------------
Total: 124 bytes  (CRC covers bytes 0-121)

All float fields are at 4-byte-aligned offsets -- required by Cortex-M0+ (no unaligned multi-byte access).

docs.title: 9.4 RFD900x Transmission
docs.text: Parameter Value Interface UART0 -> TX GPIO 28 / RX GPIO 29 Baud rate 57600 bps (TTC_BAUD) TX frequency 1 Hz nominal, 0.2 Hz in LANDING Mode Transparent serial (no MAVLink required) Packet size 124 bytes -> ~17ms at 57600 bps Redundancy Packet repeated 2x if no ground ACK within 2s

docs.title: 9.5 Repartition dual-core (implementee)
docs.text:
Core 0 (loop()) : collecte raw + telemetrie + terminal. Chaque subsystem gere son flux et recopie ses octets vers Serial sans parsing. Core 1 (loop1()) : surveillance Watchdog dediee, independante de Core 0.

void loop() {                 // ---- Core 0 ----
  watchdog.notifyAlive();     // signale la vitalite a Core 1 (surveillance croisee)
  terminal.poll();            // lecture non bloquante des commandes (USB/TT&C)

  // 1) Recopie raw de chaque subsystem vers Serial (debug)
  ibNav.forwardRaw();         // trames NMEA (I2C0, sondage 1 Hz)
  adcs.forwardRaw();          // flux ADCS  (UART1)
  jetson.forwardRaw();        // sondage I2C1 a 1 Hz

  // 2) Assemblage + transmission telemetrie au sol a 1 Hz
  static uint32_t lastTx = 0;
  if (millis() - lastTx >= 1000) {
    lastTx = millis();
    TelemetryPacket pkt = {};        // zero-init : champs non collectes = 0
    telemetry.collectOBCHealth(pkt); // etat de vol, uptime, fautes, batterie
    telemetry.buildPacket(pkt);      // sync 0xAA55 + version + CRC-16
    telemetry.transmit(pkt);         // 124 octets vers RFD900x (TT&C)
  }
}

void loop1() {                // ---- Core 1 ----
  watchdog.kick();            // nourrit le WDT SI Core 0 vivant, sinon fullSystemReset
  watchdog.checkAllFaults();  // poll des 5 pins FAULT + escalade

  // Surveillance CLK : ADCS(1)/Jetson(2)/A7Sii(4) generent un signal d'activite.
  // IbNav(0)/TTC(3) renvoient true (pas de CLK en entree).
  for (uint8_t i = 0; i < 5; i++)
    if (!watchdog.monitorHeartbeat(i)) watchdog.faultHandler(i);

  delay(1000);                // Core 1 dedie : delay acceptable a 1 Hz
}

Securite inter-coeurs : si loop() (Core 0) se bloque, notifyAlive() n'est plus appele ; apres CORE0_ALIVE_TIMEOUT_MS (4s), kick() cesse de nourrir le WDT et declenche fullSystemReset(). Detection d'un blocage Core 0 en ~4-8s. Si les deux coeurs gelent, le WDT hardware (8s) reboot seul.

Source Methode Mecanisme Liaison IbNav (GPS) IbNav::forwardRaw() requestFrom(IBNAV_ADDR, 32) @ 1 Hz (timer interne), puis recopie I2C0 ADCS ADCS::forwardRaw() while (available()) Serial.write(read()) UART1, texte brut Jetson Jetson::forwardRaw() requestFrom(0x42, 8) @ 1 Hz (timer interne), puis recopie I2C1 Telemetrie collectOBCHealth -> buildPacket -> transmit timer 1 Hz, paquet 124 B UART TT&C (RFD900x)

Flux en texte brut : pas de sync bytes, pas d'accumulation, pas de parsing binaire. Les octets recus (UART ADCS, ou I2C IbNav/Jetson) sont recopies tels quels. L'ancienne structure binaire ADCSPacket et les sync bytes 0xAD 0xC5 ont ete retires.

Non bloquant : IbNav::forwardRaw() et Jetson::forwardRaw() embarquent chacun leur propre timer millis() 1 Hz (sondage I2C), donc loop() ne bloque jamais. ADCS lit son UART de maniere non bloquante (available()).

Telemetrie implementee : collectOBCHealth() remplit flightState + uptime + faultFlags ; buildPacket() ajoute sync/version/CRC-16 ; transmit() envoie les 124 octets via TTC::transmit() sur l'UART RFD900x.

Prochaine etape : implementer collectGPS / collectADCS / collectJetson (parsing des flux texte vers les champs de TelemetryPacket) et la lecture battVoltage via une broche ADC dediee.

docs.title: 10.1 States & Transitions
docs.text:
[BOOT] --> [ASCENT] --> [APOGEE] --> [DESCENT] --> [LANDING]
State Trigger Detection BOOT Power on Startup init complete ASCENT After boot Vertical speed > +1 m/s sustained APOGEE Peak altitude Vertical speed crosses 0, altitude max DESCENT Balloon burst Vertical speed < -2 m/s sustained LANDING Ground contact Altitude < threshold + speed ~ 0 for >30s

Detection source: IbNav GPS altitude + computed vertical speed (Deltaaltitude / Deltatime)

docs.title: 10.2 Subsystem Power Profile per State
docs.text:
Subsystem BOOT ASCENT APOGEE DESCENT LANDING IbNav ON ON ON ON ON ADCS OFF ON ON ON OFF Jetson OFF ON ON OFF OFF TT&C ON ON ON ON ON A7Sii OFF ON ON OFF OFF

TT&C always active -- critical ground link in all phases. IbNav always active -- needed for localization and recovery.

docs.title: BOOT
docs.text:
• Estimated duration: < 30s
  • Objective: GPIO init, fault check, TT&C handshake with ground
  • Watchdog: active but tolerant (long delays, no power cycles during init)

docs.title: ASCENT
docs.text:
• Estimated duration: 90-120 min (0 -> ~30 km)
  • All subsystems active
  • A7Sii recording
  • Jetson processing science data
  • TT&C telemetry rate: nominal (1 Hz)
  • Watchdog: fully active, all faults monitored
  • IbNav PPS monitored: GPS loss -> immediate alert

docs.title: APOGEE
docs.text:
• Estimated duration: a few minutes around altitude peak
  • Detection: vertical speed ~ 0 +/- 0.5 m/s over 10s sliding window
  • A7Sii: highest priority (maximum field of view)
  • TT&C: burst telemetry -- transmit compressed science data
  • Watchdog: normal

docs.title: DESCENT
docs.text:
• Estimated duration: 30-60 min (burst -> ~5 km/min)
  • Jetson OFF -> power and thermal savings
  • A7Sii OFF
  • ADCS active for parachute stabilization
  • TT&C: recovery beacon active (GPS position repeated)
  • Watchdog: IbNav critical, fault -> immediate power cycle

docs.title: LANDING
docs.text:
• Detection: altitude < 500m + speed < 1 m/s for 30 consecutive seconds
  • Jetson OFF, ADCS OFF, A7Sii OFF
  • IbNav: broadcast position every 10s via TT&C
  • Power: maximum savings mode
  • Watchdog: relaxed (no aggressive power cycles on ground)
  • RP2040 WDT: always active to prevent freeze during recovery wait

docs.title: 11.1 Problem
docs.text: The TPS1H200 FAULT pins are purely binary: HIGH (nominal) or LOW (fault). They give no direct information about altitude or speed. The strategy is to cross-correlate multiple binary sources and apply temporal analysis to infer flight state.

docs.title: 11.2 Binary Signals Available on OBC
docs.text: Signal GPIO Type What it can indicate PPS_IbNav 4 Pulse 1 Hz GPS lock active -> valid position F1_IbNav 5 Fault LOW IbNav power problem F1_ADCS 11 Fault LOW ADCS power problem F1_Jetson 17 Fault LOW Jetson overcurrent (CPU load) F1_TTC 22 Fault LOW TT&C power problem F1_A7Sii 26 Fault LOW Camera power problem

docs.title: A -- PPS Temporal Analysis (GPIO 4)
docs.text:
The GPS PPS signal is a precise 1 Hz pulse when fix is valid.

Measure: interval between two rising edges of PPS

PPS present + stable interval ~1000ms -> GPS lock -> altitude available via IbNav I2C
PPS absent                             -> GPS lost -> fall back to time-based

Implementation: pulseIn(PIN_PPS_IBNAV, HIGH) or rising-edge interrupt + timestamp.

docs.title: B -- Fault Count on Sliding Window
docs.text:
A single binary fault means little. Its frequency over a time window is informative:

10s window -- Jetson fault count:
  0 faults   -> subsystem stable, normal load
  1-2 faults -> transient overload (intensive compute -> apogee?)
  >3 faults  -> real problem -> watchdog escalates

docs.title: C -- Cross-Channel Correlation
docs.text:
Flight state generates consistent load patterns across multiple subsystems simultaneously:

Observed pattern Probable state All nominal, PPS stable Normal ASCENT Jetson transient fault + A7Sii nominal Apogee data processing F1_Jetson LOW permanent + PPS ok Jetson overconsumption -> descent (cold, low battery) F1_ADCS LOW after shock LANDING (parachute impact) All nominal + PPS lost Altitude > 40 km (GPS signal loss) -- near apogee

docs.title: D -- Fault Duration Analysis (time-in-fault)
docs.text:
Brief fault  (< 200ms)  -> transient, TPS1H200 auto-retry sufficient
Medium fault (200ms-2s) -> overload, software power cycle
Long fault   (> 5s)     -> failure -> escalate + change power profile

Measure with millis() at F1 falling edge and rising edge.

docs.title: E -- Hybrid State Machine: binary + time
docs.text:
Without parsed GPS, state can be inferred via mission timer + subsystem health:

t=0          -> BOOT
t > 60s + all subsystems nominal        -> ASCENT (assumed)
t > 5400s (90 min) OR repeated Jetson faults -> APOGEE (assumed)
Jetson permanent fault + brief ADCS fault    -> DESCENT (burst detected)
Brief F1_ADCS LOW + all silent > 30s         -> LANDING

This mode is the fallback if IbNav I2C is not yet parsed.

docs.title: 11.4 Recommended Detection Architecture
docs.text:
Level 1 (primary)   : Parse IbNav I2C -> altitude + vspeed GPS
                      Accurate ASCENT/APOGEE/DESCENT/LANDING detection

Level 2 (secondary) : PPS timing -> confirms GPS lock
                      Consistent with Level 1

Level 3 (fallback)  : Mission timer + binary fault patterns
                      Used if IbNav I2C silent or GPS lost

2-of-3 vote         : If Level 1 and Level 3 diverge -> keep current state,
                      log divergence via TT&C, do not change state

docs.title: 11.5 Additional Useful Signals (if hardware permits)
docs.text: Signal Source Value Accelerometer ADCS ADCS UART Balloon burst detection (brief acceleration spike), landing impact RP2040 internal temp Internal ADC Altitude correlation (cold = high altitude) Battery voltage ADC GPIO 26-29 Power health, coherent with flight phase TT&C RSSI Via TT&C serial Ground signal loss -> confirms high altitude

docs.title: Contexte
docs.text: Le Watchdog détecte les fautes sur 5 sous-systèmes via des broches FAULT actives-basses (TPS1H200 open-drain, INPUT_PULLUP sur RP2040). Deux niveaux de test : 1. Tests unitaires — logique testée en isolation avec des mocks logiciels, sans hardware 2. Tests hardware — simulation de fautes sur le vrai Pico par loopback GPIO

docs.title: 1. include/subsystems/PowerChannel.h
docs.text:
Rendre isFault() virtuelle pour permettre l'override par un mock : 

virtual bool isFault() const;
Coût : 1 pointeur vtable (4 bytes) × 5 instances = 20 bytes sur 264 KB RAM.

docs.title: 2. include/behavior/Watchdog.h
docs.text:
Ajouter l'injection de canaux. En production _channels == nullptr → comportement inchangé. 

#include "subsystems/PowerChannel.h"

// dans la classe :
void setChannels(PowerChannel** channels, uint8_t count);

// private :
PowerChannel** _channels;      // nullptr = production globals
uint8_t        _channelCount;

docs.title: 3. src/behavior/Watchdog.cpp
docs.text:
init() — initialiser les nouveaux membres : 

_channels     = nullptr;
_channelCount = 0;

Ajouter setChannels() : 

void Watchdog::setChannels(PowerChannel** ch, uint8_t n) {
  _channels = ch; _channelCount = n;
}

checkAllFaults() — brancher sur _channels : 

PowerChannel* def[] = { &ibNav, &adcs, &jetson, &ttc, &a7sii };
PowerChannel** ch   = _channels ? _channels : def;
uint8_t        cnt  = _channels ? _channelCount : 5;
for (uint8_t i = 0; i < cnt; i++) { ... }

docs.title: Structure des tests
docs.text:
test/
├── unit_watchdog/
│   ├── MockPowerChannel.h
│   └── test_main.cpp
└── hw_watchdog/
    └── test_main.cpp

docs.title: MockPowerChannel (test/unit_watchdog/MockPowerChannel.h)
docs.text:
#pragma once
#include "subsystems/PowerChannel.h"

class MockPowerChannel : public PowerChannel {
public:
  explicit MockPowerChannel(const char* name)
    : PowerChannel(0, 0, name), _faultState(false) {}

  void setFault(bool s) { _faultState = s; }
  virtual bool isFault() const override { return _faultState; }

private:
  bool _faultState;
};
- Les pins 0,0 sont des valeurs fictives — digitalRead/digitalWrite ne sont jamais appelés - Pas d'allocation dynamique, pas de STL

docs.title: Tests unitaires (test/unit_watchdog/test_main.cpp) — 14 cas
docs.text:
Pattern setUp : wdt.init(8000) + wdt.setChannels(channels, 3) + reset des 3 mocks

ID Fonction Condition testée TC-INIT-01 init() _prevFaults remis à 0 (vérif indirecte via transitions suivantes) TC-INIT-02 init() _faultCounters remis à 0 TC-INIT-03 init() _disabledMask remis à 0 (tous les canaux pollés) TC-KICK-01 kick() pas de crash sans WDT hardware TC-KICK-02 kick() safe si appelé 10× consécutifs TC-CHECK-01 checkAllFaults() aucun log si tous les canaux sont sains TC-CHECK-02 checkAllFaults() transition sain→faute détectée sur 1 canal TC-CHECK-03 checkAllFaults() transition faute→CLEAR détectée TC-CHECK-04 checkAllFaults() 2 canaux en faute simultanément TC-CHECK-05 checkAllFaults() canal désactivé (_disabledMask) est skippé TC-CHECK-06 checkAllFaults() oscillation faute/clear sur 3 cycles TC-CHECK-07 checkAllFaults() pas de FAULT dupliqué en steady-state TC-CHECK-08 checkAllFaults() getName() correct dans le log Serial TC-CHECK-09 checkAllFaults() count=0 ne crash pas

docs.title: Câblage loopback requis (5 fils)
docs.text:
Canal Broche FAULT Broche driver Action IbNav GPIO 5 GPIO 19 driver LOW → isFault() = true ADCS GPIO 11 GPIO 25 driver LOW → isFault() = true Jetson GPIO 17 GPIO 27 driver LOW → isFault() = true TTC GPIO 22 GPIO 28 driver LOW → isFault() = true A7Sii GPIO 26 GPIO 29 driver LOW → isFault() = true

Les broches FAULT TPS1H200 sont open-drain. Quand la puce n'est pas alimentée, la broche flotte et INPUT_PULLUP la tient à HIGH. Le driver RP2040 peut la tirer à LOW sans contention.

docs.title: Cas de test
docs.text: ID Ce qui est vérifié TC-HW-01 Baseline : toutes les broches FAULT lisent HIGH (pas de faute) TC-HW-02 Loopback IbNav : driver LOW → ibNav.isFault() = true TC-HW-03 Loopback ADCS TC-HW-04 Loopback Jetson TC-HW-05 Loopback TTC TC-HW-06 Loopback A7Sii TC-HW-07 checkAllFaults() détecte FAULT + CLEAR réels (canal TTC) TC-HW-08 5 canaux en faute simultanément → 5 FAULT, puis 5 CLEAR TC-HW-09 Pullup tient HIGH quand driver en INPUT (fil débranché simulé) TC-HW-10 kick() ne corrompt pas l'état de faute

docs.title: Configuration platformio.ini
docs.text:
[env:test_unit]
board          = pico
upload_port    = COM10
test_port      = COM10
test_speed     = 115200
test_framework = unity
test_build_src_filter =
    +<behavior/Watchdog.cpp>
    -<boot/>
    -<subsystems/>
    -<main.cpp>

[env:test_hw]
board          = pico
upload_port    = COM10
test_port      = COM10
test_speed     = 115200
test_framework = unity
test_build_src_filter =
    +<*>
    -<main.cpp>
  • test_unit : exclut les .cpp des sous-systèmes pour éviter les globaux hardware (ibNav, adcs…)
  • test_hw : inclut tout sauf main.cpp (conflit avec le point d'entrée Unity)

docs.title: Commandes
docs.text:
pio test -e test_unit        # tests logiciels (pas de câblage nécessaire)
pio test -e test_hw          # tests hardware (câblage loopback requis)
pio test -e test_unit -v     # verbose : voir les [FAULT]/[CLEAR] en live

docs.title: Note sur la capture Serial dans Unity
docs.text: PlatformIO génère automatiquement un unity_config.c qui redirige unityOutputChar() → Serial.write(). Les lignes [FAULT] / [CLEAR] émises par checkAllFaults() apparaissent dans le flux mais sont ignorées par le parser Unity (format incorrect). Elles restent visibles en mode -v. Ne pas appeler Serial.begin() dans les tests — Unity le gère.
